assessed_clauses.original_text: by mutual written consent of Parent and the Company;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by mutual written consent of Parent and the Company;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Bilateral Termination by Mutual Written Consent Anytime
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: none - mutual consent terminations typically do not trigger termination fees or break-up fees for either party, as both sides are agreeing to walk away; parties generally bear their own expenses
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows either party to terminate the agreement by mutual consent, which is a standard provision that poses very low risk of deal failure as it requires agreement from both parties.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by either Parent or the Company, if the Effective Time shall not have occurred on or before January 14, 2027 (the "Initial Outside Date"); provided, that if on the Initial Outside Date (or, if the Initial Outside Date shall have been extended pursuant to this Section 8.01(b), if on the then-scheduled Outside Date, as applicable) any of the conditions set forth in Section 7.01(b) (to the extent relating to the matters set forth in Section 7.01(c) or any Antitrust Law) or Section 7.01(c) shall not have been satisfied but all other conditions set forth in Article VII shall have been satisfied or waived (or shall then be capable of being satisfied if the Closing were to take place on such date), then either Parent or the Company may, by written notice to the other party, extend the Initial Outside Date in successive three (3)-month increments until no later than January 14, 2028 (the Initial Outside Date, as it may be extended pursuant to this Section 8.01(b), the "Outside Date"); provided, that the right to terminate this Agreement under this Section 8.01(b) shall not be available to any party whose failure to fulfill any agreements and covenants under this Agreement has been the principal cause of, or resulted in, the failure of the Effective Time to occur on or before such date;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by either Parent or the Company, if the Effective Time shall not have occurred on or before January 14, 2027 (the "Initial Outside Date"); provided, that if on the Initial Outside Date (or, if the Initial Outside Date shall have been extended pursuant to this Section 8.01(b), if on the then-scheduled Outside Date, as applicable) any of the conditions set forth in Section 7.01(b) (to the extent relating to the matters set forth in Section 7.01(c) or any Antitrust Law) or Section 7.01(c) shall not have been satisfied but all other conditions set forth in Article VII shall have been satisfied or waived (or shall then be capable of being satisfied if the Closing were to take place on such date), then either Parent or the Company may, by written notice to the other party, extend the Initial Outside Date in successive three (3)-month increments until no later than January 14, 2028 (the Initial Outside Date, as it may be extended pursuant to this Section 8.01(b), the "Outside Date"); provided, that the right to terminate this Agreement under this Section 8.01(b) shall not be available to any party whose failure to fulfill any agreements and covenants under this Agreement has been the principal cause of, or resulted in, the failure of the Effective Time to occur on or before such date;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Mutual Outside Date Termination with Automatic Regulatory Extensions
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Termination under outside date provisions typically triggers reverse termination fees if regulatory approvals are not obtained, protecting the target from buyer's failure to secure clearances. The automatic extension mechanism reduces the likelihood of fee payment by giving additional time for regulatory resolution.
red_flags: Potential for regulatory delays causing extension of outside date
Conditions for extension could lead to prolonged uncertainty
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows either party to terminate the agreement if the closing does not occur by the specified outside date, with provisions for mutual extensions based on regulatory conditions, which could introduce delays and uncertainty in deal completion.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by either Parent or the Company, if any Governmental Authority of competent jurisdiction shall have enacted, issued, promulgated, enforced or entered any Law or Order permanently enjoining or otherwise prohibiting or making illegal the consummation of the Merger and such Law or Order shall have become final and nonappealable, or if there shall be adopted following the date of execution of this Agreement any Law that makes consummation of the Merger illegal or otherwise prohibited; provided, that the party seeking to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(c) has fulfilled its obligations under Section 6.06;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by either Parent or the Company, if any Governmental Authority of competent jurisdiction shall have enacted, issued, promulgated, enforced or entered any Law or Order permanently enjoining or otherwise prohibiting or making illegal the consummation of the Merger and such Law or Order shall have become final and nonappealable, or if there shall be adopted following the date of execution of this Agreement any Law that makes consummation of the Merger illegal or otherwise prohibited; provided, that the party seeking to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(c) has fulfilled its obligations under Section 6.06;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Permanent Legal Restraint Preventing Merger Consummation Termination Rights
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Typically no termination fee payable by either party when termination occurs due to final governmental prohibition, as this represents a regulatory failure outside parties' control; however, the proviso requiring compliance with efforts obligations protects against strategic behavior that might trigger the prohibition.
red_flags: Potential for regulatory intervention to block the merger
Final and nonappealable nature of the law or order could lead to immediate termination
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows either party to terminate the agreement if a government authority enacts a law that permanently prohibits the merger, posing a significant risk if regulatory challenges arise.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by either Parent or the Company, if this Agreement shall fail to receive the Company Stockholder Approval at the Company Stockholders' Meeting (or any adjournment or postponement thereof at which a vote is taken on the Merger);
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by either Parent or the Company, if this Agreement shall fail to receive the Company Stockholder Approval at the Company Stockholders' Meeting (or any adjournment or postponement thereof at which a vote is taken on the Merger);
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Termination Right Upon Failure to Obtain Required Stockholder Approval
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Generally no termination fee payable as this is a mutual termination right based on stockholder decision rather than party breach or fiduciary change; however, 3 instances show fee-bearing characteristics suggesting some agreements may impose fees if vote failure results from target's failure to recommend or other conduct
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows either party to terminate the agreement if the required stockholder approval is not obtained, which poses a moderate risk of deal failure if shareholder support is uncertain.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by Parent, if, prior to the receipt of the Company Stockholder Approval, the Company Board shall have effected, and not withdrawn at least five (5) Business Days prior to the Company Stockholder Meeting, an Adverse Recommendation Change;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by Parent, if, prior to the receipt of the Company Stockholder Approval, the Company Board shall have effected, and not withdrawn at least five (5) Business Days prior to the Company Stockholder Meeting, an Adverse Recommendation Change;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Fiduciary Out Termination Rights for Adverse Recommendation Changes
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Exercise of this termination right typically triggers payment of a termination fee (reverse break fee when acquirer terminates due to target's recommendation change, or target break fee when target terminates due to acquirer's change). The fee compensates the non-breaching party for deal costs and lost opportunity.
red_flags: Potential for the target's board to change recommendation under pressure
Timing of the recommendation change could coincide with shareholder dissatisfaction
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows the target's board to terminate the agreement if they change their recommendation against the merger, creating a significant risk of deal failure if the board perceives a better alternative.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by the Company, at any time prior to the time at which the Company Stockholder Approval is obtained, if the Company Board determines to enter into an Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal in accordance with Section 6.03; provided that, (i) the Company shall not have breached (other than a de minimis breach) Section 6.03 with respect to such Superior Proposal and (ii) prior to or substantially concurrently with, and as a condition to the effectiveness of such termination, the Company pays to Parent the Company Termination Fee;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by the Company, at any time prior to the time at which the Company Stockholder Approval is obtained, if the Company Board determines to enter into an Acquisition Agreement with respect to a Superior Proposal in accordance with Section 6.03; provided that, (i) the Company shall not have breached (other than a de minimis breach) Section 6.03 with respect to such Superior Proposal and (ii) prior to or substantially concurrently with, and as a condition to the effectiveness of such termination, the Company pays to Parent the Company Termination Fee;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Target Board Termination Right to Accept Superior Proposal
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Target must pay termination fee (typically 2-4% of deal value) to the original acquirer prior to or concurrently with termination. This fee compensates the jilted buyer for deal costs and opportunity costs. 37 of 43 clauses explicitly require fee payment, and 20 indicate this may be the sole remedy available to the acquirer.
red_flags: Potential for the target to accept a Superior Proposal, leading to deal termination
Requirement for payment of a termination fee may not sufficiently deter target from pursuing better offers
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows the target company to terminate the agreement to accept a superior proposal, which poses a significant risk to the acquirer as it could lead to deal failure if a more attractive offer is presented.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by Parent, if the Company shall have breached any of its representations or warranties, or failed to perform any of its covenants or agreements set forth in this Agreement, which breach or failure to perform (i) would give rise to the failure of a condition set forth in Section 7.02(a) or Section 7.02(b) and (ii) is incapable of being cured prior to the Outside Date or, if curable by such date, is not cured within the earlier of (A) thirty (30) calendar days after written notice thereof is given by Parent to the Company and (B) the Outside Date; provided that Parent shall not have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(g) if Parent is then in breach of any of its representations or warranties or Parent or Merger Sub is then in breach of any of its covenants or agreements hereunder, in either case, such that any condition in Section 7.03(a) or Section 7.03(b) is not satisfied or capable of being satisfied by the Outside Date;
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by Parent, if the Company shall have breached any of its representations or warranties, or failed to perform any of its covenants or agreements set forth in this Agreement, which breach or failure to perform (i) would give rise to the failure of a condition set forth in Section 7.02(a) or Section 7.02(b) and (ii) is incapable of being cured prior to the Outside Date or, if curable by such date, is not cured within the earlier of (A) thirty (30) calendar days after written notice thereof is given by Parent to the Company and (B) the Outside Date; provided that Parent shall not have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(g) if Parent is then in breach of any of its representations or warranties or Parent or Merger Sub is then in breach of any of its covenants or agreements hereunder, in either case, such that any condition in Section 7.03(a) or Section 7.03(b) is not satisfied or capable of being satisfied by the Outside Date;
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Termination Rights for Material Breach with Cure Period
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Termination under these provisions typically does not trigger termination fees unless the breach is willful or intentional. May preserve rights to specific performance or damages for material breach. The non-breaching party generally walks away without fee obligation, though the breaching party may face liability for damages.
red_flags: Potential for breach claims by the target if issues arise close to the Outside Date
Cure period may not be sufficient for complex breaches
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows the acquirer to terminate the agreement if the target breaches its representations or warranties and fails to cure the breach before the Outside Date, posing a moderate risk of deal completion failure if the target is unable to remedy issues in time.

assessed_clauses.original_text: by the Company, if Parent shall have breached any of its representations or warranties, or Parent or Merger Sub shall have failed to perform any of its covenants or agreements set forth in this Agreement, which breach or failure to perform (i) would give rise to the failure of a condition set forth in Section 7.03(a) or Section 7.03(b) and (ii) is incapable of being cured prior to the Outside Date or, if curable by such date, is not cured within the earlier of (A) thirty (30) calendar days after written notice thereof is given by the Company to Parent and (B) the Outside Date; provided that the Company shall not have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(h) if the Company is then in breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements hereunder such that any condition in Section 7.02(a) or Section 7.02(b) is not satisfied or capable of being satisfied by the Outside Date.
assessed_clauses.processed_text: by the Company, if Parent shall have breached any of its representations or warranties, or Parent or Merger Sub shall have failed to perform any of its covenants or agreements set forth in this Agreement, which breach or failure to perform (i) would give rise to the failure of a condition set forth in Section 7.03(a) or Section 7.03(b) and (ii) is incapable of being cured prior to the Outside Date or, if curable by such date, is not cured within the earlier of (A) thirty (30) calendar days after written notice thereof is given by the Company to Parent and (B) the Outside Date; provided that the Company shall not have the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 8.01(h) if the Company is then in breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements hereunder such that any condition in Section 7.02(a) or Section 7.02(b) is not satisfied or capable of being satisfied by the Outside Date.
assessed_clauses.cluster_theme: Termination Rights for Material Breach with Cure Period
assessed_clauses.fee_implications: Termination under these provisions typically does not trigger termination fees unless the breach is willful or intentional. May preserve rights to specific performance or damages for material breach. The non-breaching party generally walks away without fee obligation, though the breaching party may face liability for damages.
red_flags: Potential for termination if acquirer breaches, which could be interpreted broadly
Cure period may not be sufficient to address significant breaches
assessed_clauses.termination_explanation: This clause allows the Company to terminate the agreement if the Parent breaches its obligations and fails to cure the breach within a specified timeframe, which poses a moderate risk to deal completion if the acquirer is unable to meet its representations or covenants.
